text.title: Core Vision Rules and Gotchas
text.summary: Detail setting selection, caching by image hash, API metadata limitation, and critical agent gotchas.

rule.statement: Use detail="low" for quick image classification; use detail="high" for layout-heavy documents. Mismatching costs 4x tokens unnecessarily.
rule.rationale: OpenAI's detail setting controls how many 512px tiles are processed. "low" uses a single fixed tile (85 tokens); "high" tiles the full image (up to ~1700 tokens for a 1024px image). Using "high" for a simple yes/no classification quadruples cost with no accuracy benefit.

rule.statement: Cache extraction results keyed on SHA-256 of the image bytes, not the filename. Re-scanning the same image should never re-call the API.
rule.rationale: Filenames are reused across pipeline runs. A content hash is stable regardless of how the file is named or moved.

rule.statement: VLMs cannot read image metadata (EXIF, PDF author, creation date). Extract metadata with Pillow or PyMuPDF in a separate step; do not ask the VLM for it.
rule.rationale: VLMs see only the rendered pixel content of an image. EXIF data, file creation timestamps, and PDF document properties are invisible to them.

rule.statement: Treat handwritten text extraction as low-confidence and always require human review. Printed text below ~100px height also degrades significantly.
rule.rationale: VLMs are not fine-tuned as OCR engines. Handwriting accuracy varies widely; small printed text below 100px can be misread character by character.

list.item: Base64 encoding of a 5MB JPEG becomes ~7MB of text — monitor request size to avoid payload cap failures.
Anthropic API image size cap is 5MB (not 20MB like OpenAI). Pre-check before routing to Claude models.
For financial or legal document extraction, require human review when confidence is "low" or extracted amounts exceed a business threshold before writing to any system of record.
VLMs cannot see file metadata (EXIF, PDF author) — use Pillow or PyMuPDF for metadata extraction.
